theme: Light Mode for Customer/Admin, Optional Dark for KDS
archetype: Archetype 1 (Organic & Earthy) for Customer App / Archetype 4 for Dashboards

customer_menu.style: Bento Grid (Mode A) for featured items, List view for standard items
customer_menu.cards: Flat background, 1px border, large appetizing images, generous padding
customer_menu.cart: Sticky bottom bar, glassmorphism, high contrast checkout button

kitchen_dashboard.style: Control Room Grid (Mode B), Kanban style columns (Pending, Prep, Ready)
kitchen_dashboard.cards: Dense, tech-focused, large text, high contrast status indicators
kitchen_dashboard.interaction: Touch-friendly drag/drop or large tap targets for 'Bump' actions

admin_dashboard.style: Sidebar navigation, card-based KPIs, data tables for menu management
admin_dashboard.cards: Grid Borders look, 1px borders, white background, no shadows

forms.style: Shadcn UI customized, no generic focus rings, use subtle background shifts

instructions_to_main_agent: Strictly follow the color palette, using Terracotta (#E05D36) for primary actions and Moss Green (#4A7C59) for success states.
Implement the customer QR menu using a mobile-first design with a sticky bottom cart using crystal glassmorphism (backdrop-blur-xl bg-white/70).
The Kitchen Dashboard should use a Kanban-style layout (Pending, Preparing, Ready) with large, touch-friendly cards and clear typography.
Use Outfit for all headings and Manrope for body text. Ensure all headings have tracking-tight applied.
Avoid using generic shadows. Use subtle 1px borders (border-border) to define containers and cards.
Ensure all interactive elements have data-testid attributes (e.g., data-testid='add-to-cart-button').
For the admin dashboard, utilize Shadcn UI components but strip away heavy shadows in favor of a clean, 'Grid Borders' look.
The KDS should optionally support a dark mode or high-contrast theme for better visibility in a kitchen environment.
Add 'data-testid' to all buttons, links, form inputs, menus, and order cards.
